title: Home
text:
title: “Dr. Sonali Sharma” subtitle: “Geospatial Analyst | Remote Sensing | Urban Forest Analytics” —
I turn raw spatial data into decisions — individual trees from point clouds, carbon stocks from allometric equations, urban growth from satellite time series. Seven years of international research across India, Germany, and Finland have given me hands-on experience spanning LiDAR processing, high-resolution land cover mapping, machine learning, ecosystem service modelling, and reproducible geospatial workflows at city and regional scale.
I am open to GIS consultancy, remote sensing, environmental planning, and research roles — and always interested in collaborative projects where spatial analysis can drive decision-making and real-world impact.
📧 ssonalipduoh@gmail.com  ·  LinkedIn  ·  Download CV

title: Home
text:
Projects

🌳 LiDAR and Urban Forest Analytics
Helsinki open LiDAR · Python · rasterio · laspy · scikit-image
Fully automated pipelines from raw .laz point cloud to individual tree inventories, carbon stock maps, and GeoAI accuracy assessments. Built on open Helsinki City LiDAR data; every step is reproducible.



Notebook
Description
Methods




Urban tree detection
Automated individual tree inventory from raw LiDAR
CHM, watershed, prominence filter


AGB and carbon mapping
Wall-to-wall carbon stock map
Repola allometrics, IPCC Tier-1


GeoAI vs LiDAR urban
Deep learning height model validation, urban site
SSL model, R2, residual maps


GeoAI vs LiDAR forest
Same validation for forested site
Bias analysis, spatial error






🗺️ Land Use / Land Cover and Urban Growth
ArcGIS · QGIS · Google Earth Engine · Python · R
Multi-temporal LULC mapping and urban growth scenario modelling across cities in the Western Himalaya — integrating climate, land cover, and remote sensing datasets to understand how cities expand and what they displace.
Published in Land (2022) and Ecological Indicators (2022).
Notebooks and maps coming soon



🌿 Ecosystem Services Mapping
GEE · Python · R · ArcGIS
Spatial quantification of soil erosion, flood regulation, urban heat mitigation, and carbon sequestration across urban gradients. Delivered as geospatial model outputs and planning-ready map products in collaboration with municipal and research partners.
Published in City and Environment Interactions (2020) and Energy, Ecology and Environment (2020).
Notebooks and maps coming soon



🤖 Machine Learning for Urban Green Spaces
Transfer learning · Python · aerial imagery · Helsinki
Applied transfer learning to characterise private urban green spaces from high-resolution aerial imagery — distinguishing managed gardens, overgrown plots, and semi-natural patches at city scale. Conducted at the University of Helsinki in collaboration with Stockholm University.
Under review in Urban Forestry and Urban Greening.
Code releasing on paper acceptance
